case_name: Gary Merchant v. Corizon Health, Inc.

summary.brief: Gary Merchant v. Corizon Health, Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving emergency medicine.

evidence_index.text: Merchant alleges that he swallowed a razor blade to force his transport to a hospital because Corizon was providing him inadequate medical care.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Several hours after arriving at the hospital, doctors diagnosed a necrotizing fasciitis infection in Merchant’s left leg and determined that an above-the-knee leg amputation was necessary to save his life.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Merchant submitted several Health Services Requests (HSRs) to Corizon.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On January 22, 2016, he submitted an HSR reporting a Chron’s Disease “flare-up” and requesting Prednisone.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Merchant first complained of leg pain in an HSR dated February 2.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A Corizon nurse evaluated Merchant and observed “+4 pitting edema” on both legs.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Merchant “refuse[d] to be seen” on February 4 and “[was] angry for unknown reasons.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around 12:30 a.m. on February 7, Merchant told one of the emergency room doctors that “he’[d] been experiencing a Crohn’s flare-up . . . and just couldn’t take the pain anymore.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The emergency room doctors admitted Merchant to the intensive care unit (ICU) based on several concerns.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The panel affirmed the district court’s exclusion of expert evidence under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 37(c)(1).
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The district court did not err in granting summary judgment to defendants because plaintiff failed to demonstrate a genuine issue of material fact for trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We also conclude the district court correctly found that Merchant failed to exhaust his administrative remedies as required by the Prison Litigation Reform Act (PLRA).
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Merchant repeatedly failed to meet his disclosure obligations, the district court reasonably concluded Merchant’s failures were not substantially justified or harmless.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The district court sanctioned Merchant by excluding his improperly disclosed expert evidence under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 37(c)(1).
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The district court also considered the appropriateness of lesser sanctions, despite Merchant’s failure to move for such, and found that Merchant’s “chronic” noncompliance prejudiced Corizon.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Swallowed razor blade to force hospital transport
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Merchant alleges inadequate medical care by Corizon, leading to self-harm to receive hospital treatment.

focused_exam.legs: Left calf red, swollen, and bruised

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation for infection source

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to hospital after swallowing razor blade

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in hospital with suspected infection
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Order imaging to assess leg infection
options.clinical_reasoning: Imaging is necessary to confirm necrotizing fasciitis
Delayed imaging can lead to progression of infection

expected_action_court.description: Imaging should have been ordered immediately
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Prompt imaging is standard for suspected necrotizing fasciitis

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in imaging led to progression of infection
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Imaging should have been performed to confirm diagnosis
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for suspected severe infection

key_findings: Plaintiff failed to demonstrate a genuine issue of material fact
Plaintiff failed to exhaust administrative remedies

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to hospital after swallowing razor blade

allegations.allegation: Failure to timely diagnose and treat leg infection

court_findings.finding: Plaintiff failed to exhaust administrative remedies
